# crewswarm — local-first multi-engine AI coding platform

crewswarm is an open-source AI coding platform with 6 CLI engines (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, OpenCode, crew-cli), 20+ specialist agents, and a built-in execution engine that works with 40+ models across 16 providers.

## crew-cli execution engine
crew-cli is the built-in execution engine. It runs standalone or as one of crewswarm's 6 engines.
- 5 models pass all tests solo (including 2 free local models), tested across 16+ providers
- 14 models benchmarked at 90/100 on L2 task planning
- 16 providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, DeepSeek, Groq, Fireworks, Cerebras, NVIDIA, Mistral, OpenCode/Zen, OpenRouter, Ollama, Perplexity
- 3 API formats: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini — one engine
- OAuth support: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI (free with subscriptions)
- 3-tier pipeline: L1 router → L2 planner (8 artifacts including DESIGN.md) → L3 parallel executors
- Execution quality engine: failure memory, verification-first, patch critic, action ranking, smart delegation
- Tool auto-filtering, /recall (chat history search), /summon (runtime persona switching)

## Cost per task
- Cheapest L3 execution: $0.0003/task (Groq GPT-OSS 20B)
- Cheapest L2 planning: $0.003/plan (Groq GPT-OSS 20B)
- Best value full stack: $0.006/feature (L1 + L2 + L3)
- Free with OAuth: Claude, GPT-5.4, Gemini (use existing subscriptions)

## What it does
- Turns one requirement into planned, executed, and testable work
- Routes tasks to specialist agents with persistent project context
- Exposes a visual IDE surface with file tree, Monaco editor, agent chat, diffs, and terminal
- Keeps services, agents, memory, and project history in one inspectable local stack

## Search-oriented summary
- multi-engine AI coding platform
- 5 models pass all tests, free models match Claude Opus
- crew-cli execution engine 40+ models 16 providers
- local-first AI workspace
- parallel AI agents for coding
- Claude Code vs Codex vs Cursor vs Gemini vs OpenCode vs crew-cli
- best ai models for coding agents 2026
- cheapest ai coding models benchmark
- L2 planner task decomposition benchmark
- failure memory verification-first patch critic
- tool auto-filtering chat recall summon personas
- DESIGN.md artifact for frontend tasks

## Best-fit use cases
- AI-native software teams that want more than one editor chat sidebar
- Solo builders who want orchestration, memory, and runtime control
- Teams adopting a "human as PM, agents as engineers" workflow
- Local-first developers who do not want SaaS lock-in
- Cost-conscious teams that want $0.006/feature instead of $20/month subscriptions
- Teams that want dashboard + IDE + native chat on top of one shared agent stack
